articles.title: 3 Polish Stocks On The Radar
articles.description: Poland is becoming harder for investors to ignore. As companies across Europe rethink supply chains, manufacturing footprints and logistics strategy, Poland is emerging as one of the region's most important markets for long term industrial and infrastructure exposure. The country's investment case is being driven by more than low costs. Poland offers a mix of strong domestic demand, rising foreign investment, nearshoring appeal and strategic logistics advantages, making it one of the more compelling structural growth stories in Europe. That could keep names like CD Projekt, Orlen and KGHM Polska Miedź on investor watchlists as capital continues shifting toward Central Europe. Why It Matters Poland is no longer just a lower cost manufacturing market. It is increasingly becoming a strategic European hub for: Nearshoring Industrial production Warehouse expansion Logistics infrastructure Energy and materials exposure That matters because investors looking across Europe are increasingly focused on markets that combine: Relative economic resilience Supply chain relevance Skilled labor Infrastructure investment Long term corporate reinvestment Poland checks many of those boxes. Poland's Investment Case Is Built On Scale And Positioning Poland's economy has become one of the more resilient stories in Europe, supported by strong domestic consumption and continued foreign direct investment. Private consumption contributes roughly 57.8% of nominal GDP, a sign that the country's growth profile is not purely export driven. Foreign direct investment also remains strong. In 2025, inflows reached roughly $33 billion, up nearly 15% year over year, while almost 94% of foreign companies reportedly reinvest profits locally. That kind of reinvestment matters because it suggests foreign capital is not just entering Poland. It is staying there. For investors, that strengthens the case that Poland is becoming a durable part of Europe's long term industrial map rather than a temporary cost advantage trade. Nearshoring Could Be One Of Europe's Biggest Poland Trades One of the strongest structural themes supporting Poland is nearshoring. As manufacturers in Germany and the Benelux region look to bring parts of their supply chains closer to the European Union, Poland is increasingly being viewed as a preferred destination. That makes sense. The country sits at the center of major north south and east west trade corridors and borders seven countries, including Germany. It also offers: More than 5,000 kilometers of expressways and motorways Roughly ... Full story available on Benzinga.com

articles.title: Call for vote on inquiry into claims Starmer lied over Mandelson dismissed by Labour grandees – UK politics live
articles.description: Alan Johnson and David Blunkett say Tory proposal for a privileges committee inquiry is a ‘nakedly political stunt’ Good morning. Kemi Badenoch is trying to get Lindsay Hoyle, the speaker, to give MPs a vote on a proposal to get the Commons privileges committee to investigate allegations that Keir Starmer lied to MPs in statements he made to them about the vetting of Peter Mandelson. Other opposition parties may be backing her, but we don’t know for sure because the process is relatively secret; MPs have to write a private letter to the speaker , who then decides whether this is a serious request that should be decided by the Commons as a whole, or a frivolous complaint that should be ignored. (We do know that Karl Turner has written to the speaker about this too, but only because he was daft enough to post his letter on social media last week .) Today we are likely to find out whether or not Hoyle is agreeing to a Commons vote. Boris Johnson was referred to the privileges committee over allegations that he lied to MPs about Partygate (allegations the committee concluded were justified). Badenoch wants to make the case that Starmer is just as dishonest as Johnson. He isn’t, by any stretch, and the claims that Starmer lied to MPs about Mandelson are spurious; they relate to contest intepretations of political language of the kind that are commonplace in parliamentary debate. But the fact that this has even become a live consideration for the speaker is a big win for the Tories. The fact that Kemi Badenoch has changed the accusations she is levelling against the PM on an almost daily basis as her claims have failed to stand up to scrutiny shows what this is really about. This is a nakedly political stunt with no substance ahead of the May elections . Any comparison with Boris Johnson is absurd. When parliament referred that matter to the privileges committee, a police investigation had directly disproved his categoric statements that he knew nothing about the breach of lockdown rules. I suppose our constituents might ask [if a privileges committee goes ahead], have we got the balance right between holding the government to account and seemingly squabbling amongst ourselves when there is so much else going on that perhaps parliament ought to be focusing on as well. I have to say, a really truthful position is, why the rush at the moment? Has it got anything to do with local elections? Continue reading...
